column1: DATA BROKER REMOVAL TRACKING SYSTEM — HOW TO USE THIS WORKBOOK
US-Focused | 93 Data Brokers | 9 Paid Services | Built June 2026

column3: Start here. Read before using.
High-level progress view. Shows completion %, broker counts by tier and status. Update automatically as you fill in the Master Tracker.
The core of the system. One row per broker. Fill in columns N–U as you work through each removal. This is your primary working document.
Comparison of 9 automated removal services. Use this to decide which service(s) to subscribe to as your 'always-on' layer.
Log every search you perform on each broker site. Record whether your data was found, what was found, and the profile URL. Critical for tracking.
Recurring opt-out schedule. Data reappears every 3–6 months. Use this to plan quarterly re-checks.

Go to the 🤖 Paid Services tab. Choose at least one automated service (recommendation: Incogni + Optery together for maximum coverage). Subscribe and set up your profile. This handles the bulk of brokers automatically.
Go to 🎯 Master Tracker. Filter by 'Tier 1 – Crucial'. These 16 brokers are the most visible and must be done manually even if you use a paid service. Work through them in order.
For each broker, go to the site, search for yourself, and log the result in 🔍 Search Log. Record whether data was found and the profile URL. This is your evidence trail.
Follow the opt-out method in column H of the Master Tracker. After submitting, update the Status (col N), Date Submitted (col O), and Confirmation # (col P).
After the processing time (col J), return to the site and verify your data is gone. Update Verified Removed (col Q) and Date Verified (col R).
Data reappears. Use the 📅 Maintenance Calendar to schedule quarterly re-checks. Most brokers need re-checking every 3–4 months.
You have not yet visited this broker or submitted an opt-out.
You searched and your data was NOT present. No action needed now.
Your data IS present. Opt-out has not yet been submitted.
You have submitted the opt-out request and are waiting for processing.
You have verified that your data has been removed from this broker.
Data was removed but has re-appeared. Re-submit opt-out.
This broker is covered by your automated removal service subscription.
Not applicable to you, or you have chosen to skip this broker.
Highest visibility. These appear on page 1 of Google for name searches. Do these FIRST, manually, regardless of paid service coverage.
Major marketing, financial, and background check data brokers. Critical for reducing spam, insurance impacts, and employer background checks.
Remaining people search sites, specialty databases, and direct mail lists. Work through these after completing Tiers 1 and 2.
Opt-out is NOT permanent. Most brokers re-add your data within 3–6 months from public records. This is an ongoing maintenance task, not a one-time project.
When opt-outs require email confirmation, use a secondary or masked email (e.g., SimpleLogin, Apple Hide My Email). Some brokers use confirmation emails for future marketing.
No single paid service covers all brokers. The best strategy is: 1 automated service + manual Tier 1 opt-outs + periodic manual sweeps of the full list.
Many brokers own others. Opting out of Intelius, for example, covers ~20 subsidiary sites. Check the 'Also Covers' column in the Master Tracker.
Freezing your credit (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion, Innovis, ChexSystems) is a separate action from data broker opt-outs. Both are necessary.
